title: Avoid dynamic allocation on real-time paths
description: Use static or pool allocation for anything in a real-time or ISR path so timing is deterministic and the heap cannot fragment or fail.
body: Use when code runs in an ISR, a high-priority task, or any path with a deadline. Do not call malloc/free (or new/delete) on these paths — allocation time is non-deterministic and the heap can fragment until it fails on a long-running device. Actions: pre-allocate buffers statically or from fixed-size pools at init, size them for the worst case, and inspect real-time paths for hidden allocations (including library calls). Validation signals include a deterministic measured execution time and a long-running soak test with no allocation failure. Risk boundary: a heap failure or fragmentation pause on a device with no operator is unrecoverable; require static allocation on safety- or deadline-critical paths and escalate any heap use there.
